raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Constructing temporary sleeping cabins used to shelter the homeless will be less expensive with the governor's approval of a measure that allows these temporary shelters to be built without fire sprinklers but with alternative fire safety standards.",
    "More than 173,000 Californians experience homelessness based on the January 2022 Point in Time homeless count.",
    "The cost of adding plumbing necessary for sprinklers adds an expense that limits the number of units an agency or community group can provide.",
    "AB 42 allows for the creation of more temporary housing to serve the homeless in a safe manner.",
    "AB 42 prohibits, until January 2027, a local agency from imposing or enforcing requirements to provide fire sprinklers in temporary sleeping cabins if certain conditions are met.",
    "Cabin sites must have a 24-active fire watch at the site regardless of spacing between the cabins.",
    "Cabins must be constructed of noncombustible material.",
    "Smoke and carbon monoxide alarms as well as fire extinguishers must be in each unit of the temporary cabins.",
    "Cabin sites must prohibit the use of open flames, smoking, and combustibles.",
    "AB 42 will allow nearly double the number of units of temporary housing to be created to help the unhoused community."
  ]
}
```
